Embedded Systems

Course Code: ENGR 301 (Draft)

Term: Fall 2026

Level: Undergraduate

# Embedded Systems (Draft) *This course is currently under development. Detailed content, including lecture slides and lab manuals, will be uploaded soon.* --- ## 📘 Course Description This course provides a comprehensive introduction to the design and implementation of embedded systems. Students will gain hands-on experience with popular microcontrollers, learning to interface with sensors, actuators, and communication protocols. ### Key Topics - **Introduction to Microcontrollers**: Memory architectures, GPIO, and interrupts. - **Real-Time Concepts**: Task scheduling and RTOS fundamentals. - **Communication Protocols**: Deep dive into I2C, SPI, and UART. - **Embedded Security**: Secure boot and hardware-based trust. - **Low-Power Optimization**: Techniques for battery-powered IoT devices. --- ## 📂 Course Resources (Coming Soon) - [ ] **Syllabus**: Download PDF (TBA) - [ ] **Lecture 1**: Introduction to Embedded World - [ ] **Lab 1**: Getting Started with Cortex-M - [ ] **Project Guide**: Autonomous Rover Design --- *For inquiries about this course, please contact the instructor.*
